skill.name: qa-test-planner
skill.description: Create QA test plans, manual test cases, regression suites, Figma validation checklists, and structured bug reports.
skill.summary: Plan QA coverage and produce structured testing documentation for product releases.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but most Markdown and weak-cryptography hits are false positives from documentation, examples, and templates. Manual review found a real command execution risk in two bundled shell scripts because they use eval on interactive user input. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious exfiltration intent was found.

high_findings.title: Shell eval on Interactive Input
high_findings.description: The helper scripts assign prompt responses with eval. A user-provided value containing shell command substitution can execute commands when the script processes input. This appears to be unsafe implementation rather than confirmed malicious intent.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both scripts pass interactive input through eval. The variable names are hardcoded, but the input value is re-parsed by the shell, which is a direct command execution vector.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Many static external command findings point to fenced examples and template text in Markdown files. These locations document prompts or output formats and do not execute commands by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown examples or test case templates. They are not executable shell code unless a user manually copies unrelated text into a shell.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography findings reviewed here point to skill descriptions, headings, and QA documentation. No cryptographic algorithm implementation or weak hash usage was found in those cited locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain prose and headings. They do not call MD5, SHA1, DES, or other cryptographic APIs.

low_findings.title: Example URLs and QA Criteria Are Not Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Network and system reconnaissance detections correspond to example URLs, pass or fail criteria, and placeholder QA content. They do not contain network scanning commands or automated probing logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is QA documentation. No evidence found of curl, wget, port scanning, credential collection, or remote execution in these locations.

low_findings.title: Figma Token Setup Requires User Credential Care
low_findings.description: The Figma validation guide mentions configuring an API token for MCP access. This is legitimate for design validation, but users should rely on secure MCP configuration and avoid placing tokens in generated test artifacts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The guide references API token setup but does not include code that reads, stores, or transmits secrets. The concern is operational handling, not confirmed secret exposure.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell eval on Interactive Input
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper scripts assign prompt responses with eval. A user-provided value containing shell command substitution can execute commands when the script processes input. This appears to be unsafe implementation rather than confirmed malicious intent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Both scripts pass interactive input through eval. The variable names are hardcoded, but the input value is re-parsed by the shell, which is a direct command execution vector.

content.value_statement: QA teams need clear test documentation before releases, but manual formatting slows planning. This skill generates structured test plans, cases, regression suites, design checks, and bug reports.
actual_capabilities: Creates structured test plans with scope, strategy, entry criteria, exit criteria, risks, and deliverables.
Generates manual test cases with preconditions, steps, expected results, test data, and execution status fields.
Builds smoke, targeted, sanity, and full regression suite outlines with priority guidance.
Provides Figma validation checklists for layout, typography, color, spacing, states, and responsive behavior.
Produces bug report formats with reproduction steps, environment details, evidence fields, impact, and verification notes.
Includes two interactive shell helpers for creating Markdown test cases and bug reports.
limitations: It does not execute tests or verify application behavior automatically.
Figma validation requires a configured Figma MCP server and design file access.
Generated outputs still need review against product requirements and release priorities.
The bundled shell scripts should be fixed before publication because they use eval on user input.

use_cases.title: Prepare Release Test Coverage
use_cases.description: Create a test plan and regression checklist for a new feature before QA execution starts.

use_cases.title: Write Manual Test Cases
use_cases.description: Convert feature requirements into step-by-step manual test cases with expected results.

use_cases.title: Document Reproducible Bugs
use_cases.description: Turn observed defects into consistent reports with severity, environment, evidence, and verification sections.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Test Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for feature-level QA planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use qa-test-planner to create a test plan for the account signup feature. Include scope, test types, entry criteria, exit criteria, risks, and deliverables.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Manual Test Cases
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for executable QA steps.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use qa-test-planner to generate eight manual test cases for checkout. Cover happy path, validation errors, payment failure, promo codes, and guest checkout.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Regression Suite
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for release or hotfix validation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use qa-test-planner to build a targeted regression suite for changes to the payment module. Group tests by smoke, P0, P1, and exploratory checks.

prompt_templates.title: Validate UI Against Figma
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for design implementation review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use qa-test-planner to create a Figma validation checklist for the login page. Include layout, spacing, colors, typography, states, responsiveness, and bug report criteria.

output_examples.input: Create a test plan for password reset.
output_examples.output: A test plan with scope, objectives, environments, entry and exit criteria, risks, required test data, and deliverables.

output_examples.input: Generate manual test cases for profile editing.
output: A valid update test with expected saved changes.
Validation tests for missing and invalid values.
Permission and session tests for protected profile data.

output_examples.input: Document a bug where checkout loses the cart after refresh.
output_examples.output: A structured bug report with severity, priority, environment, reproduction steps, expected behavior, actual behavior, impact, evidence fields, and verification tasks.

best_practices: Provide requirements, user roles, environments, and known risks before asking for a test plan.
Review generated test cases with product and engineering owners before execution.
Keep Figma links, screenshots, logs, and build numbers attached to related bug reports.
anti_patterns: Do not accept generated tests without checking feature-specific business rules.
Do not use vague steps such as verify it works without expected results.
Do not place secrets, live credentials, or private tokens in test data examples.

faq.question: Does this skill run automated tests?
faq.answer: No. It creates QA documentation and planning artifacts. Test execution remains manual or handled by other tools.

faq.question: Can it create regression suites?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline smoke, targeted, sanity, and full regression suites with priorities and execution order.

faq.question: Does Figma validation work without MCP?
faq.answer: No. Figma-specific extraction requires a configured Figma MCP server and access to the design file.

faq.question: What output format does it use?
faq.answer: The guidance and helper scripts focus on Markdown documents for test plans, test cases, and bug reports.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish as reviewed?
faq.answer: No. The audit found unsafe eval usage in two shell scripts, so publication should wait until that is fixed.

faq.question: Who benefits most from this skill?
faq.answer: QA engineers, QA leads, product managers, and teams that need consistent release testing documentation benefit most.
